title: Team
text:
Meet the members of the LSU Skill Acquisition Lab.






Principal Investigator

Gal Ziv is the Principal Investigator of the LSU Skill Acquisition Lab and an Associate Professor and Senior Researcher at Lithuanian Sports University. His research focuses on motor learning and skill acquisition, with particular expertise in eye-tracking studies and perceptual-motor learning in complex tasks.
Google Scholar Profile | ResearchGate Profile





Researcher

Dalia Mickevičienė is an Associate Professor at the Department of Health Promotion and Rehabilitation and a Senior Researcher at the Institute of Sport Science and Innovation at the Lithuanian Sports University. She contributes to the strategic research field “Muscles, motor control, and health promotion” and is a member of the research group “Impact of Environmental Factors.” Her research focuses on motor control and learning, with an emphasis on understanding the development of motor skills and their interaction with environmental influences.




Researcher

[Add bio here]
Google Scholar Profile




Doctoral Student

[Add bio here]




Bachelor’s Student

Neila Danilevičiūtė is a junior specialist and research assistant at the Lithuanian Sports University, a personal trainer conducting research related to motor learning, skill acquisition, and older adults’ health.

title: Join Us
text:
We are always interested in hearing from motivated students and researchers who are passionate about motor learning and skill acquisition.



We welcome applications from:

Doctoral students: Looking to pursue a PhD in motor learning, skill acquisition, or related fields
Master’s students: Interested in thesis projects involving eye-tracking, perceptual-motor learning, or experimental motor learning research
Bachelor’s students: Seeking hands-on research experience in our lab
Postdoctoral researchers: With expertise in motor learning, cognitive neuroscience, or related areas
Visiting researchers: Interested in collaborative projects or research stays




Ideal candidates have:

Strong interest in motor learning and skill acquisition
Background in sport science, kinesiology, psychology, or related disciplines
Enthusiasm for experimental research and data analysis
Good communication skills in English
(For graduate positions) Research experience and/or relevant academic background




Our lab focuses on:

Eye-tracking and gaze behavior in complex motor tasks
Perceptual-motor learning in racing simulation
Skill acquisition in sport and performance contexts
Ecological psychology approaches to motor learning
Human information processing in dynamic environments





If you are interested in joining, please contact Gal Ziv at gal.ziv@lsu.lt
Please include:

A brief description of your research interests
Your CV
Any relevant academic transcripts (for student positions)

title: LSU Skill Acquisition Lab
text: Advancing understanding of motor learning and skill acquisition

title: LSU Skill Acquisition Lab
text:
Welcome
Welcome to our skill acquisition lab. We study how humans learn complex, often sport-related motor skills. Specifically, our research group is interested in the relationship between eye movements and performance and learning in complex tasks that require rapid information processing. We use a racecar driving simulator to examine how novices can learn a difficult perceptual-motor task based on various learning theories.

title: LSU Skill Acquisition Lab
text:
Our Research Focus
Our lab investigates fundamental questions about how people acquire and refine motor skills. We employ cutting-edge technologies including eye-tracking systems and racing simulators to understand the perceptual-motor processes underlying skill development.

title: LSU Skill Acquisition Lab
text:
Location
We are based at the Lithuanian Sports University in Kaunas, Lithuania.

title: Publications
text:
Our lab’s publications span motor learning, skill acquisition, eye-tracking, and perceptual-motor performance. For a full and up-to-date list, visit the PI’s profiles:

Google Scholar
ResearchGate

A curated publication list will be added here as the lab grows.
